title: Raffaele Imparato

infobox.value: (born 3 September 1986) is an Italian [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who plays as a [defender](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") for USD Vis Ariano Accadia.

Di Napoli, Giuseppe (4 January 2008). ["UFFICIALE: Imparato, Magliocco e Prisco alla Juve Stabia"](http://www.tuttomercatoweb.com/?action=read&id=86097). *Tutto Mercato Web* (in Italian). Retrieved 29 October 2010.

D'Ambrosio, Germano (3 July 2008). ["UFFICIALE: Salernitana, Magliocco e Imparato rescindono"](http://www.tuttomercatoweb.com/?action=read&id=111771). *Tutto Mercato Web* (in Italian). Retrieved 28 October 2010.

["Di Fatta, Imparato e Mezavilla al Taranto"](https://web.archive.org/web/20130926223316/http://www.ilcalciocatania.it/article.php?id=16770) [Di Fatta, Imparato and Mezavilla at Taranto] (in Italian). Calcio Catania. 31 July 2009. Archived from [the original](http://www.ilcalciocatania.it/article.php?id=16770) on 26 September 2013. Retrieved 26 September 2013.

["TUTTI I TRASFERIMENTI"](https://web.archive.org/web/20110218124840/http://www.lega-calcio.it/it/Serie-A-TIM/Tutti-i-trasferimenti.page). *Lega Calcio* (in Italian). 2 February 2010. Archived from [the original](http://www.lega-calcio.it/it/Serie-A-TIM/Tutti-i-trasferimenti.page) on 18 February 2011. Retrieved 29 October 2010.

["Maccarrone al Gubbio, Imparato al Milazzo"](https://web.archive.org/web/20131001220716/http://www.ilcalciocatania.it/article.php?id=23669) (in Italian). Calcio Catania. 31 August 2011. Archived from [the original](http://www.ilcalciocatania.it/article.php?id=23669) on 1 October 2013. Retrieved 26 September 2013.

**Raffaele Imparato** (born 3 September 1986) is an Italian [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who plays as a [defender](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") for USD Vis Ariano Accadia.

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Imparato was born in [Avellino](/wiki/Avellino "Avellino"), Campania. He began his senior career with the [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") club, [Sangiuseppese](/wiki/F.C._Sangiuseppese "F.C. Sangiuseppese"). He made six appearances for the club during his first season as a professional.

### Salernitana Calcio

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Salernitana Calcio")]

Ahead of the [2005–06 Serie C1](/wiki/2005%E2%80%9306_Serie_C1 "2005–06 Serie C1") season, he was signed by [Salernitana Calcio 1919](/wiki/Salernitana_Calcio_1919 "Salernitana Calcio 1919"), the team that replaced bankrupted *Salernitana Sport* and represented the town of [Salerno](/wiki/Salerno "Salerno"). In January 2006, Imparato was loaned to [Nocerina](/wiki/A.S.G._Nocerina "A.S.G. Nocerina") and made just 4 league appearances during the second half of the 2005–06 season. Ahead of the 2006–07 season, he was loaned to [Sassari Torres](/wiki/Sassari_Torres_1903 "Sassari Torres 1903"), where he made 22 league appearances in the former [Serie C2](/wiki/Serie_C2 "Serie C2"). He returned to Salerno ahead of the 2007–08 season, but after making just 8 appearances, he was again loaned out. In January 2008 he joined [S.S. Juve Stabia](/wiki/S.S._Juve_Stabia "S.S. Juve Stabia") on a 6-month loan deal, along with teammates [Roberto Magliocco](/w/index.php?title=Roberto_Magliocco&action=edit&redlink=1 "Roberto Magliocco (page does not exist)") (loan) and [Lorenzo Prisco](/wiki/Lorenzo_Prisco "Lorenzo Prisco") (permanent deal).[[2]](#cite_note-2) On 3 July 2008, along with Magliocco, Salernitana terminated the player's contracts with club,[[3]](#cite_note-3) although Salernitana won promotion to [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") as [Serie C1](/wiki/Serie_C1 "Serie C1") Group B champions.

### Calcio Catania

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Calcio Catania")]

On 10 July 2008, he was signed by [Serie A](/wiki/Serie_A "Serie A") club [Calcio Catania](/wiki/Calcio_Catania "Calcio Catania"), on a 5-year contract.[[4]](#cite_note-Catania-4) He was immediately loaned to [Lega Pro Prima Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione") side, [Paganese Calcio](/wiki/Paganese_Calcio_1926 "Paganese Calcio 1926") ahead of the [2008-09 Serie A](/wiki/2008-09_Serie_A "2008-09 Serie A") campaign.[[4]](#cite_note-Catania-4)

On 1 July 2009, Imparato returned to [Catania](/wiki/Catania "Catania") and on 31 July 2009 was transferred to [A.S. Taranto Calcio](/wiki/A.S._Taranto_Calcio "A.S. Taranto Calcio") on a season long loan deal[[5]](#cite_note-5) along with [Marco Di Fatta](/wiki/Marco_Di_Fatta "Marco Di Fatta") and [Adriano Mezavilla](/wiki/Adriano_Mezavilla "Adriano Mezavilla") who joined the club in [co-ownership deals](/wiki/Co-ownership_(football) "Co-ownership (football)"). Imparato's loan was, however, terminated on 1 February 2010.[[6]](#cite_note-6) He returned to [Catania](/wiki/Calcio_Catania "Calcio Catania") for the remainder of the campaign, although he failed to feature in any official matches for the club. Ahead of the 2010–11 statistical season, Imparato remained with Catania until January 2011, when he re-joined former club, [Paganese Calcio](/wiki/Paganese_Calcio_1926 "Paganese Calcio 1926") on another 6-month loan deal. He earned regular playing time once more with the [third division](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione") club and made 15 appearances during his stay. On 30 June 2011, he returned to Catania.

Prior to the [2011-12 Serie A](/wiki/2011-12_Serie_A "2011-12 Serie A") campaign, Calcio Catania once again loaned out Imparato, this time to [Lega Pro Seconda Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione "Lega Pro Seconda Divisione") outfit [S.S. Milazzo](/wiki/S.S._Milazzo "S.S. Milazzo").[[7]](#cite_note-7) With Milazzo, Imparato went on to make 24 league appearances, and was notably sent-off twice throughout the campaign.

### Vicenza Calcio

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Vicenza Calcio")]

On 6 July 2012, Imparato officially completed a transfer to [Lega Pro Prima Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione") outfit, [Vicenza Calcio](/wiki/Vicenza_Calcio "Vicenza Calcio") on a permanent transfer, as part of the deal that saw [Alberto Frison](/wiki/Alberto_Frison "Alberto Frison") transfer to Catania on a co-ownership basis. Vicenza Calcio have made several[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] transfers as they look to limit their stay[*[according to whom?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Words_to_watch#Unsupported_attributions "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Words to watch")*] in the [Lega Pro](/wiki/Lega_Pro "Lega Pro") following their [relegation](/wiki/Serie_B_2011-12 "Serie B 2011-12") from [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") due to a play-out loss to [Empoli F.C.](/wiki/Empoli_F.C. "Empoli F.C.") on aggregate. However Vicenza readmitted to Serie B after [U.S. Lecce](/wiki/U.S._Lecce "U.S. Lecce") was expelled.

On 2 September 2013, he left for [Sorrento](/wiki/Sorrento_Calcio "Sorrento Calcio") in temporary deal,[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) re-joining forward [Pasquale Maiorino](/wiki/Pasquale_Maiorino "Pasquale Maiorino") who left for the same club on 29 August, with forward [Roberto Esposito](/w/index.php?title=Roberto_Esposito_(footballer)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Roberto Esposito (footballer) (page does not exist)") moved to Vicenza also in temporary deal on 2 September.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### Torres

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Torres")]

On 13 August 2014 Imparato and Maiorino were signed by [Torres](/wiki/S.E.F._Torres_1903 "S.E.F. Torres 1903").[[11]](#cite_note-11) The club was re-admitted to Lega Pro on 1 August.

### Perugia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Perugia")]

He reached the highest level of his career so far in the 2016–17 season, when he played 5 games for [Perugia](/wiki/A.C._Perugia_Calcio "A.C. Perugia Calcio") in the second-tier [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B").

### Agropoli

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Agropoli")]

In January 2019, Imparato joined [Agropoli](/wiki/U.S._Agropoli_1921 "U.S. Agropoli 1921").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### Vis Ariano Accadia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Raffaele_Imparato&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Vis Ariano Accadia")]

On 11 September 2019, Imparato signed with Italian club USD Vis Ariano Accadia.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
